messages.subject: [R-jobs] Statistical Programmer (SAS or Splus/R)- Edwards Lifesciences (medical device company) in Irvine, CA
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Statistical Programmer (SAS or Splus/R)- Edwards Lifesciences (medical device company) in Irvine, CA
messages.body_plain:
About the Company:

Edwards Lifesciences (NYSE: EW) is a global leader in products and technologies to treat advanced cardiovascular disease, the global leader in acute hemodynamic monitoring and the number-one heart valve company in the world.  Headquartered in Irvine, California, Edwards has more than 5,000 employees worldwide, selling medical technologies in more than 100 countries.  Edwards Lifesciences leverages its research, design, development and marketing expertise through five main product categories: Heart Valve Therapy, Critical Care, Cardiac Surgery, Vascular Therapy, and Percutaneous Heart Valve Interventions.

For us, saving lives is not just a slogan; it's what we do. From developing devices that keep a heart pumping to creating new technology that monitor vital signs in the critical care setting, we help ensure that patients not only wake up the next morning, but also regain their activity.



Opportunity Snapshot:

Be a part of a dynamic biostatistical group using the latest statistical and programming techniques to collaborate in industry-leading cardiovascular research.  Use your skills to manage, analyze, and present clinical data from trials involving heart valve repair/replacement, peripheral vascular interventions, and critical care monitoring.  Take this opportunity to be a part of an exciting company on the cutting edge of the fight against heart disease!


Responsibilities:
* Develop, test, validate, document, maintain and execute programs in SAS or Splus/R in the Clinical Affairs department.
* Prepare, document, and review data listings, summary tables, and graphs for inclusion in clinical reports or presentations.
* Collaborate with senior statisticians to prepare analysis datasets. Ensure analysis data and programming code meet regulatory and company standards and are consistently structured to permit efficient programming, reporting, and review.
* Work with company team members and respond to data requests.
* Perform quality assurance procedures on statistical data and programs.

Requirements:
* Minimum of three (3) years of solid full-time SAS programming experience, ideally with clinical data. Senior Statistical Programmers require a minimum of five (5) years full-time SAS experience in the clinical environment.
* BS or MS degree, in Mathematics, Statistics, or Computer Science. A Master's degree in statistics is preferred.
* Knowledge of UNIX and Windows operating systems required.
* Good oral and written communication skills essential.
* In order to be considered for this position you must be authorized to work in the United States, sponsorship will not be provided for this opportunity.


How to apply:
At Edwards Lifesciences, we want you to have as much information as possible before making an important career decision. To find out more about this role and the opportunity it offers, please visit our website:

www.edwards.com

OR

Click On the link below or Cut and Paste the link below into your browser:

To apply directly to this position, please click the link below or cut and paste it into your browser:
http://www.edwards.com/Careers/reqfinder?urlvariablestring=2420

__________________________

Janine Long
Recruiter
Global Talent Acquisition
Edwards Lifesciences
949.250.3413 office
949.250.3487 fax
janine_long at edwards.com
www.edwards.com

messages.subject: [R-jobs] S-Plus or R programmer for Pharmaceutical Company
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] S-Plus or R programmer for Pharmaceutical Company
messages.body_plain:
We currently have an opening for a S-Plus/R programmer at a large
pharmaceutical company. 
Job Description:
S-Plus/ R Programmer -BS/MS in statistics - Solid S-Plus (or R) and SAS
programming skills required, minimum 1 year work experience writing
functions in S-Plus or R.  SAS experience should include writing SAS macros
and creating and manipulating ODS datasets.  Strong interpersonal and
communication skills (both oral and written) are also required, as well as
the ability to work independently.  The candidate should be proficient with
Microsoft Office tools, including facility with Excel spreadsheet functions.

This statistical programmer / analyst will support the core biotechnology
team in the analysis of data from genomics  experiments.  This individual
will work under the direction of statisticians to organize datasets, create
listings and graphs to assess the quality of the data, perform statistical
tests, and create Excel spreadsheets for exploring results of the analyses.
We are really targeting an S-Plus Programmer with good Statististical
background

If you have any interest in this position please email your resume to
Christina Kinchen at Ckinchen at smithhanley.com or call 800-684-9921 ext 220. 


Christina Kinchen

Smith Hanley Consulting

Senior Recruiter

Phone 800-684-9921 ext 220

Direct Line 407-548-0670

Fax 407-805-3020

E-Mail: ckinchen at smithhanley.com

Website: www.smithhanleyconsulting.com
<http://www.smithhanleyconsulting.com/> 

 

"Ask About Our Referral Policy"
1st Placement- $1000
2nd Placement-$2000
3rd Placement-$3000
 
-You can see what positions are available by clicking on the following link:
http://smithhan.vcgasp.com/Worldlink40SmithHanleyConsultingGroup/main.aspx?a
ction=SearchOpportunities

 

-Specializing in contract placement of Statisticians, SAS Programmers,
Clinical Data Managers/Data Coordinators, and other clinical positions
throughout the US.

messages.subject: [R-jobs] FW: Sr. SAS and R programmer needed in MA, NJ, or IL
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] FW: Sr. SAS and R programmer needed in MA, NJ, or IL
messages.body_plain:
-----Original Message-----
From: Christina Kinchen [mailto:ckinchen at smithhanley.com] 
Sent: Thursday, October 25, 2007 5:05 PM
To: 'r-sig-jobs at r-project.org'
Subject: Sr. SAS and R programmer needed in MA, NJ, or IL

SAS Programmer III needed for large Pharmaceutical company in Chicago. The
individual can work in Chicago, New Jersey or Massachusetts. The ideal
candidate has extensive industry experience with SAS and R or S-Plus
programming.
Overview:
-Back up contact for programming of tables, listings, and graphs for a
specified compound project.
-Responsible for the validation and quality assurance of any output produced
for specified projects and contributing to al project management such that
timelines are met. 
-Back up participant on Clinical Project teams representing clinical
programming efforts for that clinical team. 
-Prefer candidate with clinical experience. 
-SAS program experience required.
 
Responsibilities:
-Contribute to the programming of tables and graphs for various projects
related to genomics/biomarkers and discovery, which include in vitro and
animal pharmacology. 
-Help develop automation tools in Microsoft Excel using internal links to
Visual Basic, SAS or R; develop SAS macros and R functions for various
analysis tasks, with guidance and core code from the statisticians. 
-SAS and S-Plus/R programming experience required. 
-Discovery/pre-clinical experience will be a plus. 
-Experience in Visual Basic and Web programming (HTML, JAVA, Perl, etc.)
will be a plus. 
-This is for an experienced SAS and S-Plus/R programmer. 
-Individuals should be very proficient in generating graphs, tables and
develop automated SAS and R Macros/functions and Excel templates using the
core statistical programs and guidance from the statisticians. 
-Individuals should ideally know or be willing to quickly self-learn how to
run SAS or R programs in the background from Excel and use Visual Basic in
Excel to automate analysis tasks and create Excel templates/tools. 
-Individuals should ideally have strong experience or be willing to quickly
self-learn in creating web-based interface/tools that runs SAS and R
programs in the background. 
-Since most of this work is for projects in discovery, biomarkers and pre
clinical groups, experience in clinical trials is not required. 
 
Experience/Education:
We need very strong candidates for these openings. 
A B.S. or M.S. degree in statistics or mathematics or related field, with
coursework/experience in computer science is preferred. 
A degree in computer science, biology and related fields will also be
considered if the candidates have coursework/training in statistics and
statistical programming with the experience/proficiency in topics listed
above. 

 
If interested please send resume to Christina Kinchen at
ckinchen at smithhanley.com or call 800-684-9921 ext 220.

Christina Kinchen

Smith Hanley Consulting

Senior Recruiter

Phone 800-684-9921 ext 220

Direct Line 407-548-0670

Fax 407-805-3020

E-Mail: ckinchen at smithhanley.com

Website: www.smithhanleyconsulting.com
<http://www.smithhanleyconsulting.com/> 

 

"Ask About Our Referral Policy"
1st Placement- $1000
2nd Placement-$2000
3rd Placement-$3000
 
-You can see what positions are available by clicking on the following link:
http://smithhan.vcgasp.com/Worldlink40SmithHanleyConsultingGroup/main.aspx?a
ction=SearchOpportunities

 

-Specializing in contract placement of Statisticians, SAS Programmers,
Clinical Data Managers/Data Coordinators, and other clinical positions
throughout the US.
